summary: Merkel’s cells primarily detect light touch and sustain pressure; however, recent studies indicate their crucial role extends into sensing vibrations and possibly aiding in dynamic somatosensation processes. The core functionality centers around mechanosensing yet encompasses expanded capabilities essential for complex tactile experiences.
